title: Kaboom Crew match flow + camera shake + engine timing test coverage

stories.title: Distinct player vs bot color tint via difficulty preset patch
stories.summary: At a glance the two bombers should be visually distinct. Bootstrap forces a blue tint on `player.1` and a red tint on `bot.1` via `component.set MeshRenderer.color` on initial attach + every restart. Minimap already uses these colors; this just makes the world view match.
verification: live probe: player.1.MeshRenderer.color === '#5fa8ff' and bot.1.MeshRenderer.color === '#ff5a6e' after boot AND after a restart
attachUi + restartScene both override MeshRenderer.color on player.1 (#5fa8ff) and bot.1 (#ff5a6e); minimap markers already used these colours

stories.title: Bomb fuse pulses faster as it approaches 0
stories.summary: Project-local BombFuseSystem (or a small companion system) modulates `MeshRenderer.color` on each Bomb between two warning colors. Pulse period = max(0.08 s, fuseRemaining / 8); near the end the bomb effectively flashes white→orange. No new components, no schema changes.
verification: unit test: bomb with fuseRemaining=2.5 → slow pulse; fuseRemaining=0.2 → fast pulse
live probe: bomb visibly flashes faster as it ages
bombPulseColor(fuseRemaining) exported pure helper; period clamps Math.max(0.08, t/8) so fresh bombs pulse slowly and near-zero bombs strobe; bomb-fuse-system applies the colour each fixedUpdate

stories.title: Match concept — first bomber to 3 wins is the match winner
stories.summary: Promote the existing tally into a 'match' construct. RoundState gains `matchTarget: number` (default 3) and `matchPhase: 'in-progress' | 'won' | 'lost' | 'draw'`. RoundResolveSystem flips matchPhase the moment tally.player or tally.bot hits matchTarget. The HUD banner shows MATCH win/loss banner on top of the round banner; the auto-restart skips when the match is over (player can press R to start a new match).
verification: unit test: tally.player=3 → matchPhase='won' + autoRestart suppressed; tally.bot=3 → matchPhase='lost'
live probe: HUD shows 'MATCH WIN — first to 3' banner after the 3rd round win

stories.title: Camera shake on blast — small, decaying
stories.summary: When a BlastEvent fires, perturb the camera transform with a 0.3-second decaying noise. Implementation: a tiny project-local CameraShakeSystem reads BlastEvent transient + writes Transform.position offsets on the active camera; offsets decay each frame; resets to baseline once shake intensity drops below 0.001. Magnitude scales with blast range so a range-4 detonation is felt more than range-2.
verification: unit test: shake state ramps to non-zero on BlastEvent, decays back to 0 inside 0.4 s of fixed steps
live probe: bomb at (4,4) jitters the orthographic camera visibly

stories.title: Unit test for AGF_FRAME_SPIKE emission + cooldown
stories.summary: S86 AGF-FRAME-TIMING-SPIKE-DIAGNOSTIC shipped without a dedicated unit test (only the boot-noise budget smoke covers it). Pull the spike check into a tiny pure helper inside start.ts (or a sibling util) + unit-test it: synthetic frame sequence with one spike → exactly one emission; sustained spikes throttle to ≤ 1 emission per cooldown window.
verification: unit test asserts emission count + cooldown windowing on a fake clock
engine/runtime/frame-spike-gate.ts extracts the spike detector as a pure function; +4 unit tests cover sub-threshold / cooldown / spikeMs=0 / reset
start.ts uses createFrameSpikeGate() instead of inline lastSpikeEmittedAt — behaviour identical, now testable

stories.title: engine doctor — Recent commits section (last 10 git log entries)
stories.summary: Add a recent-commits block to DoctorReport sourced from `git log --oneline -10` (one-time, captured at doctor invocation). Useful when the agent's bug-hunt needs context for 'what changed lately'. Silently empty when git is unavailable.
verification: DoctorReport.recentCommits: { hash, subject }[] populated when run inside a git repo; empty array when git command fails
format pass renders a 'Recent commits:' section

stories.title: HUD shows current Player input state via key glyphs
stories.summary: Tiny `kaboom.input` widget at bottomLeft alongside stats: small grid of WASD + Space key glyphs that light up while the key is held. Reads pressed-key state via a new `runtime.kaboom.input()` accessor on the existing PlayerInputSystem (exposes the internal pressed set as ReadonlyArray<string>). Helps players see which keys are detected as pressed (handy for stuck-key debugging).
verification: live probe: holding D lights up the D glyph; releasing it dims; spacebar pulses on press
runtime.kaboom.input() returns the pressed key code array

stories.title: Unit test for AssetRegistry.inventory() status transitions
stories.summary: S86 AGF-ASSET-INVENTORY-PROBE shipped the API without an explicit unit test. Add coverage: pending → loaded on resolve; pending → failed on reject; invalidate() clears the status; subsequent get() restarts at pending.
verification: +3 unit tests; the existing AssetRegistry suite stays green
